mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 3% signal monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 3.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate below 4.0% indicates tight labor markets and strong wage growth expectations, which compress equity risk premiums and support forward equity valuations, producing positive 20-day SPX returns.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: SPX forward returns when UNRATE < 4.0% do not exceed baseline returns by a statistically significant margin (t-test p > 0.10), or correlation between UNRATE and 20-day returns reverses sign over any 18-month period.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth accelerates above 0.5% month-over-month, signaling robust consumer demand and corporate earnings expansion, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following RSAFS acceleration > 0.5% MoM are not significantly positive (mean return < 0.5%, t-test p > 0.15), or signal triggers false positives in > 45% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production declines more than 1.0% over three months, signaling supply-side weakness and potential stagflation risk that compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 50% of instances when INDPRO declines > 1.0% over 3 months, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts decline while unemployment rises simultaneously, indicating demand destruction and labor market deterioration that signals recession risk and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as forward-looking equity markets reprice.
candidate_trigger: HOUST month-over-month decline > 5% AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in 1 month
failure_condition: Joint signal (HOUST decline + UNRATE rise) fails to predict negative 5-day returns in > 40% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth accelerates above 200k per month while unemployment remains stable below 4.0%, signaling sustained labor demand and earnings growth that supports positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: sustained labor market strength in expansion
failure_condition: Joint signal (strong payroll growth + low unemployment) produces positive 60-day returns in fewer than 60% of instances, or interaction effect is not significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: CPI inflation rises above 3.5% while federal funds rate remains below 3.0%, creating negative real rates that erode bond yields and redirect capital to equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative real rate signal fails to produce positive 20-day returns in > 50% of instances, or the interaction term (inflation × low rates) is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) in predictive model.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth decelerates below 2.0% annualized while retail sales remain flat or negative, signaling demand exhaustion and recession risk that produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as equity markets reprice growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth < 2.0% AND RSAFS month-over-month growth < 0.2%
failure_condition: Joint demand weakness signal produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.12) in logistic regression model.
